summary: A brief collection of LinkedIn posts by Sara Blakely, highlighting her philosophy on intentionally scheduling fun to maintain balance and effectiveness, as well as brief mentions of her career journey and new venture, Sneex.

principles.statement: Schedule fun with the same intentionality as your work meetings.
principles.rationale: If you don't actively schedule fun, work will consume all your time. Having fun ultimately makes you more effective in all areas of life, including as a parent, manager, and employee.
principles.source_quote: Book fun like meetings! Otherwise you'll look up one day and realize all you've done is work. Fun makes us better parents, managers, employees, and…

signature_quotes.text: Book fun like meetings! Otherwise you'll look up one day and realize all you've done is work.
signature_quotes.context: Advice on work-life balance shared on LinkedIn.

signature_quotes.text: During most of my twenties, I was in massive search mode. I was in the wrong job.
signature_quotes.context: From an article about finding your higher purpose.

anti_patterns.description: Allowing work to passively consume all your time without intentionally making space for enjoyment.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: You will eventually look up and realize all you've done is work, and a lack of fun actually makes you a worse parent, manager, and employee.
